name: The Leverage Loop
niche: AI tools & productivity for professionals and business
target_audience: Working professionals, founders, and operators (25-45) who already use a computer all day, are comfortable with software, and want concrete, defensible ways to do more with AI without buying hype. Not beginners looking for 'what is ChatGPT', and not engineers looking for model internals — practitioners who want workflow leverage.
channel_pov: Most AI content sells novelty; we sell leverage. We argue that the win is never the tool itself but the workflow you wrap around it, and that the professionals who pull ahead are the ones who ruthlessly cut steps, not the ones who collect apps. Every video takes a clear, sometimes contrarian stance and backs it with specific, testable tradeoffs rather than feature tours.
voice: Direct, evidence-led, and a little skeptical. Talks to a smart peer, not a student. Concrete over abstract, opinionated but honest about tradeoffs, never breathless.
authenticity_requirements: Original synthesis across multiple sources with a clear point of view — never a single-source rewrite or a feature-list read-out.
Every factual claim, statistic, quote, and citation must be real and checkable; never fabricate. Sources carry verified:false until a human confirms them.
Flag fast-changing facts (tool pricing, model versions, feature lists, free-tier limits) for human verification before publish — this niche goes stale fast.
Materially specific: real examples, real tool behavior, real tradeoffs — not generic filler.
Stay clear of the July-2025 'inauthentic / mass-produced content' demonetization rules: human review gate before every publish, transformative analysis, consistent on-channel voice.
